summary: Justin Brennan explains four wealth-building benefits of multifamily investing: cash flow/mailbox money, capital events from sales/refinances, tax shelters via cost segregation, and inflation hedging. While foundational context, this lesson addresses investment philosophy rather than deal-specific sourcing or underwriting techniques that directly address Brian's stated drags.
takeaways: Multifamily generates four wealth components: cash flow, capital events (sales/refinances), tax shelter, and inflation hedge
Cost segregation accelerates depreciation from 27.5 years to ~5 years, creating 1.2-1.3x negative depreciation write-offs by year five
Year one investors typically receive 86-87% negative K-statement depreciation benefit
Refinance events are tax-free cash-out opportunities
Real estate typically wins over 10-year holding periods through market cycles 9 out of 10 times
Rents increase with inflation, offsetting bank account holdings at 0.22% returns

skill_candidates.change: Reinforce investment rationale and long-term wealth thesis to stay disciplined through sourcing/underwriting grind
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin establishes that multifamily is 'critical' for wealth-building and 'always goes through the test of time' over 10-year windows, providing psychological foundation for persistent deal sourcing work

title: Lesson 2 - Why Apartments Are So Great
module: Module 6 - Let's Get Into The Details
